codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested integration suite and pytest/Makefile wiring, but two required behaviors are not actually verified: parser extraction can be bypassed, and teardown invocation is not asserted.

issues.description: VAL-INTEG-003 requires verifying that the parser extracts tool calls, but the test passes when parsing fails as long as the response has tool-call-like text (`assert parsed is not None or has_tool_content`). This allows success without proving parser extraction/check acceptance.

VAL-INTEG-009 requires verifying cleanup/teardown is called, but this test only checks `teardown` is callable and documents expected fixture behavior; it does not assert invocation (e.g., via spy/mock/call-count or post-finalizer state assertion).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-worker skill mandates TDD with failing tests first, but this integration feature required real-model behavior discovery and iteration (including relaxing assertions after observed model output variability). The skill should clarify a pragmatic flow for real-model integration tests where strict test-first is not always practical.

Gemma 4 E2B-it produced non-standard tool-call keys (`tool_name`/`params`) during integration testing, which led to weaker assertions. This model-specific behavior is not captured in shared library docs and could cause repeated re-discovery in future integration work.

summary: Reviewed feature integration-tests-real-model (commit 725a937) via handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and git diff. Result: fail, due to two blocking requirement gaps—tool-call parsing is not strictly validated and teardown invocation is not actually asserted.
